title: The Martian
logline: After being presumed dead and left behind during a Mars mission, an astronaut must use his ingenuity and scientific knowledge to survive on the hostile planet while NASA works to bring him home.
antagonistOrConflict: Mars itself - the hostile environment, limited resources, and isolation
tone: Optimistic resilience with dark humor - maintaining hope and wit in the face of seemingly impossible odds
themes: What it means to be human

actBreaks.description: Mark establishes communication with Earth via Pathfinder, ending his complete isolation and beginning the collaborative rescue effort.

validation.ruling: Scene #49 shows Mark's successful transformation from stranded astronaut to functioning survivor through a time-lapse montage ending with the first potato sprout emerging, representing his mastery of basic survival and transition into long-term planning.

actBreaks.description: The Hermes crew votes to return to Mars using the Rich Purnell Maneuver, transforming the story from Earth-to-Mark rescue to a crew-led mission.

validation.ruling: Scene #165 shows Mission Control discovering the Hermes crew has deliberately changed course to rescue Mark against NASA orders, fundamentally shifting the story from Earth-controlled rescue to crew-initiated mission and raising stakes to maximum level.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script follows a classic survival narrative structure but with a unique dual-timeline approach. The story alternates between Mark's immediate survival challenges on Mars and NASA's bureaucratic/technical efforts on Earth. The structure is remarkably patient, allowing Mark's scientific problem-solving to drive the narrative rather than rushing toward action beats. The script's genius lies in how it makes farming potatoes and calculating orbital mechanics as dramatically compelling as traditional action sequences. Each act escalates both the technical complexity and the emotional stakes, while maintaining Mark's voice-to-camera relationship as our constant anchor.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is methodical yet engaging, using Mark's video logs to maintain momentum during technical exposition. The script accelerates significantly in the final act during the rescue sequence (scenes 227-279), but earns this acceleration through careful buildup. Scene clusters around major setbacks (the airlock explosion at scene 115, the Iris failure at scene 143) create natural rhythm breaks that prevent the survival elements from becoming monotonous.

incitingIncident.description: The crew fights through brutal storm conditions toward the MAV when a massive antenna section slams into Mark and lifts him off his feet.

validation.ruling: Scene #11 is the perfect inciting incident where Mark Watney is suddenly struck by debris and disappears into the storm, forcing the crew to abandon him and shattering his ordinary world on Mars, establishing the main survival conflict.

midpoint.description: The airlock hits the hillside, Mark's faceplate shatters, and he's thrown around inside as the airlock tumbles down the hill.

validation.ruling: Scene #115 is just an explosion aftermath, not a character transformation. Scene #113 is the true midpoint where NASA decides to publicly announce Mark's survival and the rescue mission, shifting the story from secret survival to global public effort and raising stakes significantly.

climax.description: Mark launches out of the tumbling MAV and reports visual contact with Lewis as they rocket toward each other through space.

validation.ruling: Scene #259 is dialogue setup, not the climactic action. Scene #274 shows Mark being ejected from the exploding MAV into space toward the Hermes - this is the peak physical confrontation with the ultimate obstacle and the moment of highest dramatic tension.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - MARS - TO ESTABLISH
scenes.summary: Mars eclipses the sun, then sunlight breaks across the planet's surface.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: The camera moves through Martian terrain to find the Ares 3 hab site while Lewis radios the team on Sol 18.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark Watney chips at rocks during an EVA experiment and records observations while Martinez inspects the Mars Ascent Vehicle.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Dr. Beck studies samples at his station and makes a comment about Martinez over the radio.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Martinez asks if Beck insulted him while Mark confirms it, and someone offers to turn off their radios.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Johanssen sits at her computer tracking communications and offers to shut off the radios at Lewis's command.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis cuts Mark and Martinez's radio communications and apologizes to Vogel while they discuss collecting samples with the specimen drill.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Johanssen's face goes pale as she receives a mission update and calls Lewis inside to see it.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Johanssen reports that the storm warning has been upgraded to worse conditions, and Lewis sees the storm darkening the horizon while dirt starts blowing around Mark.
scenes.turningPointNote: Storm escalation forces the mission toward abort - the inciting incident that will strand Mark

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis reads the mission update showing the storm will exceed abort force limits, and the team learns they must begin abort procedures.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - STORM - DAY
scenes.summary: The crew fights through brutal storm conditions toward the MAV when a massive antenna section slams into Mark and lifts him off his feet.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark is struck by debris - the accident that will separate him from the crew

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Martinez reaches the MAV airlock and reports the vehicle has tilted to 8 degrees and will tip at 12.3 degrees.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - STORM - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Beck reports that Watney's bio-monitor shows zero blood pressure, zero pulse rate, and normal temperature before going offline.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Vogel, Beck, and Johanssen stumble into the MAV airlock.

scenes.heading: EXT. STORM - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis searches through the storm using drill bits to probe the ground while unable to see anything.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: The crew discusses search options while Martinez reports the MAV is tilting to 11.6 degrees and one good gust will tip it.

scenes.heading: EXT. STORM - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis continues fighting through the storm while Martinez reports negative contact on proximity radar.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: The MAV lurches and begins tipping past 13 degrees while Martinez prepares one final trick before following abort orders.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Martinez fires thrusters from the nosecone array to fight against the MAV's slow tilt.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: The crew monitors the MAV's tilt at 12.9 degrees while Beck and Martinez tell Lewis she needs to return to the ship immediately.

scenes.heading: EXT. STORM - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis stands alone in the storm as Martinez confirms over radio that Watney is gone.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis radios that she's on her way back while Johanssen reports the MAV is holding at 11.5 degrees.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - AIRLOCK - CONTINUOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis enters the MAV airlock, straps herself in, and gives the verbal launch command after Martinez confirms they're ready.
scenes.turningPointNote: The crew launches and abandons Mark on Mars

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - PRESS ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: NASA Director Teddy Sanders addresses the press about the storm that forced mission abort and the quick action that saved most of the crew.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark Watney regains consciousness on Mars with alarms blaring in his helmet and discovers a jagged antenna has pierced his spacesuit.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark is revealed to be alive - the major story reversal

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark climbs a hill and sees the intact Hab but realizes the MAV launch site is empty, confirming he's been left behind.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - AIRLOCK - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark enters the Hab's airlock and removes his helmet once the airlock equalizes.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark removes his suit, examines his deep puncture wound, realizes a piece of antenna broke off inside him, and gathers medical supplies for self-surgery.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark records a video log explaining he survived Sol 18 when the antenna pierced his suit, and describes his situation for the record.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark sits in his bunk drumming his fingers and thinking during the night.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAWN
scenes.summary: The first sunlight appears on Sol 21.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark uses the toilet and studies the sealed waste bag that results from the vacuum-drying process.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark inventories food rations and notices a case labeled 'DO NOT OPEN UNTIL THANKSGIVING.'

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAWN
scenes.summary: Mark sits in darkness through the night, then gets up with purpose, finds paper and pencil, and begins writing math equations.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark decides to actively solve his survival problem through calculation

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark collects the pile of silver waste bags and carries them inside the Hab.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark fills a container with water, adds compost, then tears open waste bags and dumps their contents while gagging from the smell.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark scoops Martian dirt into a container and carries it to the airlock.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark brings the dirt container inside and dumps it in a cleared corner of the Hab.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark continues bringing dirt inside until there's a huge pile in the corner by Sol 28.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark spreads dirt over a third of the Hab floor and dumps the awful-smelling compost mixture over the Martian soil.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark cuts potatoes into quarters and plants them in orderly rows across the entire Hab, which is now covered in soil on every surface.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark begins farming - his key survival strategy is now in motion

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark packs soil on bunks and finds a data-stick containing old seventies TV shows, which he watches on the computer.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT - ROVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark removes the hydrazine tank from the Mars Descent Vehicle to make water by burning rocket fuel, despite the dangerous nature of the process.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark creates a makeshift laboratory setup with tarps and tubes, then uses a torch to burn hydrazine and produce water through chemical reactions.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark explains to the camera that he needs to burn hydrogen to make water, joking about the historically bad outcomes of lighting hydrogen on fire.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark continues the water-making process but miscalculates oxygen levels, causing an explosion that blasts him across the room.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark tells the camera he blew himself up due to forgetting excess oxygen in his calculations and shares historical trivia about JPL's founding.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark adjusts his calculations and successfully produces water without exploding, creating a rainforest-like environment in the Hab.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark succeeds in creating water - a crucial survival breakthrough

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY/NIGHT
scenes.summary: Time-lapse photography shows Mark's daily routine of making water, tending soil, and maintaining his survival systems over multiple sols.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE
scenes.summary: The scene transitions from Mars to show Earth appearing from black space with its calming blue-green colors.

scenes.heading: EXT. ARLINGTON NATIONAL CEMETERY - DAY
scenes.summary: Teddy delivers a memorial speech at Arlington National Cemetery for Mark Watney, honoring his service and sacrifice while a somber crowd listens.

scenes.heading: EXT. NASA - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent Kapoor walks past the NASA logo at the Johnson Space Center entrance.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent enters NASA's main lobby where security guards are watching television coverage of the President speaking at Watney's memorial.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - VARIOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent walks through NASA as everyone in the building watches news reports of the Watney memorial service, including footage of a female astronaut eulogizing Mark.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - TEDDY’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent meets with Teddy in his office to request satellite authorization for surveying remaining Ares 3 supplies, but Teddy refuses to authorize another mission.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mindy Park processes Vincent's satellite request for Acidalia Planitia images and discovers something shocking while examining the Hab site photos.
scenes.turningPointNote: Discovery that Mark is alive - major story shift from memorial to rescue mission

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Annie, Vincent, and Teddy examine satellite images as Vincent proves Mark is alive by pointing out cleaned solar panels, moved rover, and disassembled MDV components.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - MARS - TO ESTABLISH
scenes.summary: Mark's voice states he's going to die up there as we cut to space above Mars.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark complains to his camera about Commander Lewis's disco music collection while Vicki Sue Robinson's "Turn the Beat Around" plays.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark studies a map of Mars and calculates the 3,200 kilometer distance to the Ares 4 landing site at Schiaparelli Crater where he must launch in four years.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark removes the large battery from Rover 1 and drags it to Rover 2, contemplating where to install it.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark explains to his camera the three problems with reaching Ares 4: rovers limited to 35km range, needing to survive fifty days in marginal life support, and having no contact with NASA.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark attaches the scavenged battery to Rover 2 using a makeshift harness.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DUSK
scenes.summary: Mark test drives his modified rover with the spare battery and solar cells, which holds together despite not looking pretty.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark reports to his camera from the freezing rover that he's doubled battery life but faces a deadly heating dilemma between freezing and draining power.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark drives across Mars toward a green flag and digs up the Radioisotope Thermoelectric Generator despite remembering training against handling plutonium.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark drives the rover while sweating and shirtless, warmed by the radioactive isotope, as he plays Donna Summer's "Hot Stuff" - officially the least disco song Lewis owns.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: The rover drives toward the distant Hab while "Hot Stuff" continues playing.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - PRESS ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent gives a CNN interview explaining that Mark is conducting longer rover tests to prepare for a journey to the Ares 4 launch site for communication.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - DAY
scenes.summary: Annie walks with Vincent through NASA halls, criticizing his interview performance and complaining about paying alimony to two ex-husbands.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: NASA department heads meet to discuss Mark's 76-kilometer rover journey, with Teddy banning Vincent from future TV interviews and introducing key personnel including Mindy and Mitch.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark harvests and replants potatoes in his transformed greenhouse Hab, noting he now has 400 healthy plants but still needs to solve the communication problem.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark studies his maps at the table, has a breakthrough when he spots "Chryse Planitia," and declares he knows what he's going to do.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark discovers his solution for communication - the Pathfinder mission location

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent and Mindy track Mark's rover movement for thirteen days toward an unknown destination until Vincent realizes Mark is heading for something in Acidalia Planitia.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - BREAKROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent tears a Mars poster from the NASA breakroom wall and uses it to plot Mark's course, discovering Mark's destination and grinning with understanding.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark's rover crests a hill approaching his destination where a glint of buried metal is visible in the Martian sand.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL LOBBY - DAY
scenes.summary: Bruce meets Vincent in the JPL lobby as Vincent arrives at the Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Pasadena.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. JPL STORAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Bruce and Vincent hurry across JPL campus discussing the odds of Mark getting the 1997 device working again, with Bruce noting it lasted three times longer than expected before battery failure.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark digs on foot at the location, beginning to reveal the buried metal object.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - GARAGE
scenes.summary: Vincent and Bruce enter the JPL garage where Bruce unveils a replica of the Pathfinder spacecraft with its Lander and Sojourner Rover sections.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark uncovers the actual Pathfinder on Mars and drags the Lander to his rover, lashing it to a makeshift hitch on Sol 109.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark surgically replaces Pathfinder's battery with an environment heater while Sojourner sits nearby, completing the heart transplant-like procedure with a final click.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Pathfinder's boot sequence initiates, runs hardware checks, begins broadcasting status, and successfully acquires a telemetry signal.
scenes.turningPointNote: Pathfinder successfully boots and establishes signal - communication with Earth restored

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent, Bruce, and the JPL team see the signal acquisition message appear on their main screen as excitement builds in the room.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark watches Pathfinder's high gain antenna move and angle toward Earth, then begins dancing in celebration.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Tim Grimes shows Vincent and Bruce the incoming panoramic image from Pathfinder, revealing the Martian surface with the Hab and a note reading "YES."

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark watches as Pathfinder's camera moves toward one of his notes, focusing on the word "YES" written on paper.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark explains to his camera how to communicate using Pathfinder's 1996 camera by creating a hexadecimal alphabet system with 13-degree arcs instead of the 26-letter alphabet.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark methodically sets up cards marked "A-F" and "0-9" in a circle around the Pathfinder camera to create his hexadecimal communication system.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark sits in Johanssen's bunk scrolling through her laptop, finding an ASCII table among her files that include Zork 2 and Leather Goddesses of Phobos.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Tim consults an ASCII chart while pointing the Pathfinder camera at JPL.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: The Pathfinder camera swings from card to card on Mars as the communication system operates.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark translates the camera positions using his ASCII table to read "HOW ALIVE" and begins writing his response.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The JPL team reads Mark's response explaining he was impaled by antenna, his bio-monitor was destroyed, and the crew had reason to think him dead.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark inputs code into the rover's computer to hack twenty instructions in the operating system, linking the rover to Pathfinder's frequency as Vincent's first message appears onscreen.
scenes.turningPointNote: First direct communication established between Mark and NASA - full contact restored

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent dictates to Tim at JPL while Bruce watches, telling Mark they've been watching him since Sol 54.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Teddy, Mitch, Annie, and the NASA team watch Vincent's message cross their screens, promising rescue plans and a supply mission to keep Mark fed until Ares 4.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark reads the text about supply missions and rescue plans in his rover - his first human contact in months.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tim reads Mark's response about being glad to hear rescue plans and looking forward to not dying, then asks how the crew reacted to learning he was alive.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark reads Vincent's message revealing that NASA hasn't told the crew he's alive yet because they need them to concentrate on their mission.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tim reads Mark's profanity-filled response aloud to the JPL team, censoring the F-words as he goes.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark reads Vincent's message that everything he types is being broadcast live worldwide and types another response.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL GARAGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The JPL team reads Mark's latest response and goes pale while Tim tries not to smile.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - TEDDY’S OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Teddy explains to Mitch that he had to tell the President what a "bureaucratic felcher" is, and Mitch argues they need to tell the crew about Mark being alive.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - PRESENT - SPACE
scenes.summary: Commander Lewis stares out at space from the Hermes four months after leaving Mars when Johanssen calls her over radio.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis responds to Johanssen's radio call about the data dump being almost complete.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - CORRIDOR - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis and Martinez float through the ship toward the data dump, with Martinez excited to see pictures of his son's third birthday party.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - REC ROOM - SPACE
scenes.summary: The Hermes crew gathers for the data dump and Johanssen finds a voice message addressed to the whole crew from Mitch.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - SPACE - TO ESTABLISH
scenes.summary: Title card establishes Sol 128 on Mars.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark waters his potato plants row by row while noting in voice-over that NASA won't stop talking to him now.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark talks to camera about NASA trying to micromanage his crops and jokes about posing for pictures, calling himself the best botanist on the planet.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark positions himself outside in his suit and holds up a notecard for the camera.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Annie complains about Mark's "Fonzie" photo to the team while Teddy discusses releasing it with the rescue operation announcement.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DUSK
scenes.summary: The crops inside the HAB are destroyed in a quick slow-motion shot during depressurization.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. AIRLOCK - DUSK
scenes.summary: The airlock hits the hillside, Mark's faceplate shatters, and he's thrown around inside as the airlock tumbles down the hill.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark's survival situation becomes catastrophically worse with the HAB explosion and airlock accident

scenes.heading: INT. AIRLOCK - DUSK
scenes.summary: Mark regains consciousness bleeding in the damaged airlock, sees the collapsed HAB, and frantically searches for an air leak while trying to patch it.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. AIRLOCK/HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark navigates back to the HAB using his patched suit with fabric covering his broken faceplate and his arm computer camera for vision.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark surveys the destroyed HAB and his ruined potato crops, then begins cleaning up the disaster.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark sits in the rover trying to compose how to explain the disaster to NASA.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - CONFERENCE ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent briefs the NASA team that Mark's crops are completely dead and he now has only 200 sols of food instead of lasting until the rescue mission.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark's food situation becomes critically shortened, creating an impossible timeline for rescue

scenes.heading: INT. JPL BULLPEN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bruce and his JPL team sit shellshocked after learning about the impossible timeline, with Bruce saying they'll all need a change of clothes.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE
scenes.summary: The Hermes continues toward Earth as Martinez begins writing to Mark.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES REC ROOM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Martinez types a joking message to Mark saying they don't like him and it's roomier without him on the ship.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark reads Martinez's email and types back that he accidentally blew up the HAB but Commander Lewis' disco music survived.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES REC ROOM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Martinez reads Mark's response about the vast horizons and types a brief reply saying he'll tell the others Mark said hello.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark clears debris from the HAB and stares at the hole in the airlock.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark covers the airlock hole with canvas and duct tape, then successfully repressurizes the HAB.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark takes inventory of his remaining potatoes while wind slams the canvas patch and he tries not to think about his life depending on duct tape.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - RICH’S OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rich Purnell sleeps in his office while orbital computations run on his computer, then wakes when Mike asks about probe courses and discusses launch timelines.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent dictates a message to Mark about the Iris probe taking 414 days to reach him with enough food to last until Ares 4.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark reads the message about the Iris probe being named after the Greek goddess of rainbows and speed.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mindy reads Mark's response calling it a "Gay probe coming to save me" and puts it on the main screens.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Teddy asks if the expensive probe will be ready on time, Bruce says they need fifteen more days, and Teddy suggests skipping inspections to save time.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark explains to camera that he's now eating one ration every three days instead of three per day, dividing a pathetic meatloaf portion.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAPE CANAVERAL - DAY
scenes.summary: The Iris probe is mounted on its booster and final supplies are loaded at Cape Canaveral.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mitch presides over Mission Control and begins the launch status check while joking with Vincent about believing in God.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Teddy watches the countdown from the observation room with a prepared speech while Mitch confirms they are go for launch.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Annie paces in front of televisions during the final countdown seconds.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - CONTROL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Bruce sits with his engineers in rapt attention during the countdown.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - RICH’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Rich Purnell works on orbital calculations, completely ignoring the launch countdown.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAPE CANAVERAL LAUNCHPAD - DAY
scenes.summary: The Iris probe launches successfully with liftoff from Cape Canaveral.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mission Control cheers the liftoff but the Launcher Interface reports a shimmy problem to Mitch.

scenes.heading: INT. HULL - DAY
scenes.summary: Violent shaking rattles the payload and a critical bolt cracks inside the hull.

scenes.heading: EXT. IRIS CRAFT - DAY
scenes.summary: The first stage jettisons but catches on the craft, swinging to the side as the second stage engines ignite.

scenes.heading: INT. HULL - DAY
scenes.summary: The acceleration shears off the bolt, the payload rocks, four other bolts snap, and Iris slams into the hull.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - CONTROL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Alarms flash across Mission Control consoles as the team reports the probe spinning out of control and losing signal.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Iris probe - Mark's only hope for survival - is destroyed during launch

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - VINCENT’S OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Vincent sits motionless in his dark office until a message from Mark asking how the launch went appears on his computer.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - TO ESTABLISH
scenes.summary: Mars appears against space with a title card showing Sol 186.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAWN
scenes.summary: Mark stands outside at dawn and records a message asking Commander Lewis to tell his parents he loves his work and is dying for something beautiful if he doesn't survive.

scenes.heading: EXT. CNSA - TO ESTABLISH - DAY
scenes.summary: An establishing shot shows the China National Space Administration headquarters in Beijing.

scenes.heading: INT. CNSA - DAY
scenes.summary: Teddy and Vincent answer reporters' questions about the failed supply mission, admitting they skipped inspections to make the launch window, while a Chinese official speaks in subtitled dialogue.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - TEDDY’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Teddy listens to terms from someone on the phone, closes his eyes in relief, and agrees to whatever is being offered.
scenes.turningPointNote: China offers assistance to NASA for another rescue attempt

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - BULLPEN - DAY
scenes.summary: Bruce energetically briefs his department heads about building another Iris probe in twenty-eight days, thanks to their Chinese partners.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - SUPER COMPUTER ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Rich Purnell stares at calculations on his screen, runs the numbers again, and grins as he realizes his results are correct.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - HALLWAYS - DAY
scenes.summary: Rich hurries through NASA hallways.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - VINCENT’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Rich barges into Vincent's office during a phone call, insists Vincent hang up immediately, and hands him a summary of his work.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The NASA team gathers for a secret meeting called Project Elrond, making Lord of the Rings jokes, as Rich begins to explain he can get the Hermes back to Mars by a certain Sol.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rich Purnell reveals his maneuver to return Hermes to Mars

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY/DUSK
scenes.summary: Mark trudges through his routine but stops cleaning solar panels, sits on a hill unable to continue, ignores his temperature alarm as the sun sets, then spots something metallic glinting.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mission Control observes Mark conducting systematic EVAs in a pattern, going out 300 meters at a time, and receives a transmission that appears as jumbled numbers and data.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mark addresses the camera while testing rock samples, explaining he's completing the research schedules for all five crew members from their aborted mission.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Vogel finishes engine diagnostics and finds a strange email from his wife titled "Our Children" with an unreadable attachment.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES GYM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Vogel approaches Johanssen in the gym and tells her he has a problem.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES GYM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Johanssen examines Vogel's email attachment and discovers it contains math equations for something called the "Rich Purnell Maneuver" with course instructions for the Hermes.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES REC ROOM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis briefs all five Hermes crew members on the Rich Purnell Maneuver mission that would extend their journey 211 days to rescue Watney, explaining NASA rejected it and they would be defying orders.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hermes crew decides whether to mutiny against NASA to rescue Watney

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Mission Control receives an unscheduled message from Hermes declaring "Rich Purnell is a steely-eyed missile man" followed by alarms as they discover Hermes has deliberately changed course.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hermes crew executes the Purnell Maneuver, forcing NASA's hand

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - TEDDY’S OFFICE - DAWN
scenes.summary: Teddy confronts Mitch about giving the crew the maneuver information, warning that he may have killed the whole crew, while Mitch defends passing along information.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS
scenes.summary: Sunlight creeps across the Schiaparelli Crater on Sol 219 as drilling sounds are heard.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark explains to the camera that the Ares-4 MAV is already waiting at Schiaparelli Crater 3,200 kilometers away, and the plan is to launch him into orbit for the Hermes to catch.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark drills seven hundred holes around the edge of Rover Two's roof using a rock sample drill, then uses a screwdriver and rock to break through between the holes.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY - BEGIN MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Mark positions his partially-disassembled rover in front of the camera to document his modifications.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA ROVER ROOM - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Vincent and NASA engineers study Mark's photograph and begin sketching plans for the next steps.

scenes.heading: EXT. CNSA - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Teddy and Mitch meet with Chinese officials Zhu and Guo at the China National Space Administration lobby and shake hands.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - KITCHEN - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Mark inventories his remaining ration packs while eating a potato, labeling special packs as "Departure," "Birthday," and "Last Meal."

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: The Hermes approaches Earth, slowing its rotation.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - VIDEO BOOTH - REC ROOM - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Martinez video calls his upset wife Marissa, who's angry about the 533 additional days, but eventually forgives him and they touch hands to their screens.

scenes.heading: INT. JPL - WHITE ROOM - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Bruce and his JPL team load the Iris 2 Probe into shipping containers while Chinese CNSA officials observe.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - VIDEO BOOTH - REC ROOM - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Lewis video calls her husband Robert, who delights her by showing an original 1973 ABBA's Greatest Hits album he found at a flea market.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUNCH PAD - CHINA - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: The Iris 2 Probe is attached to its booster at the Chinese launch pad.

scenes.heading: INT. CNSA - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Mitch argues with the Chinese Flight Director about protocol while Zhu and Teddy watch the heated exchange.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Mark seals a tent to Rover 2's roof and pressurizes it to check for leaks.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - WORK ROOM - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Vincent and NASA engineers work with identical equipment to figure out how to fit the Oxygenator into the pop tent.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES FLIGHT DECK - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Vogel entertains his wife and children over video call, flipping between gravity zones while the kids laugh.

scenes.heading: INT. CNSA - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: The Taiyang Shen launches successfully as Mitch, Teddy, and Chinese scientists celebrate and shake hands.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Mark drives Rover 2 across Martian dunes while bobbing his head to music.

scenes.heading: EXT. IRIS 2 PROBE - DAY (STOCK)
scenes.summary: The Iris 2 probe separates from its booster rocket in space.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES FLIGHT DECK - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Martinez takes control of the probe and pilots it towards the Hermes.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: The probe approaches the Hermes as the docking procedure begins.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 3 - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Beck, in his EVA suit and tethered to the wall, guides the probe to the docking port.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT HERMES - REC ROOM - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Johanssen watches anxiously through a window as Beck secures the docking.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 3 - SPACE - MONTAGE
scenes.summary: Beck continues guiding the probe to the docking port while secured in his EVA suit.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE - END MONTAGE
scenes.summary: The Hermes, now rotating again, soars through space on its return journey to Mars, leaving Earth behind.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS
scenes.summary: The Red Planet appears on screen with a title indicating Sol 461, over six months later.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Both rovers are now hitched together with Pathfinder mounted on top, creating a caravan-like vehicle for Mark's journey across Mars.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: The Hab has been stripped for parts and modified, with canvas sections cut and reglued making it lopsided.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark, now gaunt and bearded after six months, explains to the camera that by commandeering the Ares 4 lander without permission, he'll technically become a space pirate.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark shaves his beard, organizes boxes of experiments he kept alive, performs final shutdown of all Hab systems, and everything goes dark.

scenes.heading: EXT. HAB - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark depressurizes the Hab and stares at it for a moment, silently thanking it for keeping him alive.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark opens Rover 2 to reveal it's packed with frozen potatoes and scavenged equipment, then tosses in his final rations labeled "Goodbye, Mars."

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark climbs into Rover 1 and powers up the system to begin his journey.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark drives his rover caravan away from the Hab site toward the horizon, beginning his final journey to the Ares 4 MAV.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark begins his final journey to the Ares 4 MAV for rescue

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - VARIOUS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark walks across Mars while reflecting in voice-over about being the first person to visit various locations on the planet, leaving footprints in red dirt and using Phobos to navigate.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark sits outside the rover in his EVA suit waiting for solar panels to charge, taking in the view of Phobos moving through the sky.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Vincent and Mindy discuss Mark's progress on satellite view, noting his schedule and that he's asked to be called 'Captain Blondebeard.'

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: The rover slowly crosses Mars at 25 kph on Sol 494, the only moving thing on the entire planet.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark watches the horizon from inside or near the rover.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - VINCENT’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Bruce and Mitch present Vincent with MAV modification plans, explaining they need to make it 5,000 kilograms lighter by stripping components so Mark can reach intercept velocity with Hermes.
scenes.turningPointNote: NASA reveals the dangerous MAV modification plan that will make Mark's escape attempt extremely risky

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mindy reads Mark's profanity-laced response to the MAV plan while Vincent and others try to interpret whether he's excited or angry.

scenes.heading: INT. ROVER - ARES 4 MAV SITE - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark addresses the camera on Sol 538, expressing distress about NASA's plan to launch him in what he calls a convertible with no controls.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark stands at the base of the MAV holding a large wrench like a weapon, staring up at it with determination as ABBA's 'Waterloo' begins.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark tears the acceleration chairs out of the MAV cockpit.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: The acceleration chairs hit the dirt in a growing pile outside the MAV.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark tears out the control panels from inside the MAV, enjoying the destruction.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: The control panels hit the dirt as the pile of discarded equipment grows larger while 'Waterloo' plays.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Martinez runs a flight simulation on Hermes that ends in failure with 'Collision with Terrain' appearing on screen, prompting Lewis to make him try again.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV COCKPIT - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark waits in the MAV airlock surrounded by stripped equipment.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: The exterior of the MAV now looks like a junkyard as Mark wrenches free one of the hull panels.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: The nose airlock breaks free from above and tumbles toward the camera, blacking out the frame.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DUSK
scenes.summary: Mark sits exhausted on a hill at dusk surveying the transformed MAV, which now has its front torn off and covered with hab canvas, surrounded by scattered equipment.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE
scenes.summary: The Hermes stops rotating as it approaches Mars on Sol 560.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES REC ROOM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis briefs the Hermes crew on the rescue plan, assigning Martinez to fly the MAV, Johanssen to monitor ascent, and Beck to retrieve Watney using a 214-meter tether line.

scenes.heading: INT. POP UP TENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark eats his last ration pack in silence inside a makeshift pressurized tent on Sol 561.

scenes.heading: EXT. NASA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Global media crews create chaos outside NASA as they cover the rescue attempt.

scenes.heading: EXT. TIMES SQUARE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: New Year's Eve-level crowds gather in Times Square to watch news reports about the Watney rescue on the Jumbotron.

scenes.heading: INT. MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Teddy, Vincent, and Annie watch from Mission Control as Vincent explains they can't help due to the 24-minute communication delay, and Annie worries about broadcasting a potential failure worldwide.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREETS - BEIJING - DAY
scenes.summary: Crowds in Beijing watch screens as Johanssen's voice reports perfect engine alignment over comms.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. PETERSBERG - DAY
scenes.summary: Crowds brave cold weather to watch screens in St. Petersburg's Palace Square as Johanssen reports communications are functioning.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis keys the console and tells Watney they're about two minutes from launch, asking how he's doing.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - COCKPIT - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark tries to control his emotions in the MAV cockpit, telling Lewis he's eager to get up to her and thanking the crew for coming back, his voice breaking.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis warns Mark about the heavy G-forces and tells him it's okay to pass out, while Mark jokes about no barrel-rolls as Lewis begins the launch checklist with Johanssen and Martinez.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck floats in the open airlock above Mars while Vogel is clamped behind him as they report ready for the rescue operation.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis continues the launch checklist, calling for pilot confirmation.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - COCKPIT - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark, strapped into his acceleration seat in his EVA suit, gives the final 'Go' confirmation from the MAV cockpit.

scenes.heading: EXT. VARIOUS - DAY/NIGHT
scenes.summary: Crowds around the world erupt in cheers when they hear Mark's voice giving the go-ahead.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lewis announces over comms that Hermes is go for launch and will proceed on schedule with 10 seconds to launch.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Martinez reports main engine start as Johanssen counts down and Lewis tells Watney to hang on for the final seconds.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark tenses and tells Lewis he'll see her soon as Johanssen finishes the countdown and liftoff occurs, slamming Mark back into his seat.
scenes.turningPointNote: The MAV launches with Mark aboard, beginning the most dangerous part of his rescue attempt

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUNCHSITE - DAY
scenes.summary: The MAV launches upward with unprecedented force, accelerating faster than any manned ship in space travel history.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark struggles to remain conscious as the ship shakes violently and the hab canvas covering the nosecone begins to rip from the acceleration forces.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Johanssen reports velocity and altitude readings while Lewis notes they're too low and Martinez says the ship is fighting him, as they lose contact with Watney.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark barely conscious sees the canvas rip completely free, revealing Mars' red atmosphere that gives way to black space and stars before he passes out.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Johanssen reports Mark is well below target altitude as the MAV shuts down, with Beck explaining Mark likely passed out from 12 G's, and Johanssen calculating intercept velocity at 11 meters per second but distance at 68 kilometers.
scenes.turningPointNote: The launch has failed to reach proper altitude, creating a critical 68-kilometer gap that threatens the rescue

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck expresses shock at the 68-kilometer distance from the airlock.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis tells the crew to work the problem as Martinez confirms the MAV has no fuel left, forcing them to go to Mark using Hermes attitude thrusters despite needing fuel for the trip home.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE
scenes.summary: The Hermes attitude thrusters fire and the ship changes course toward Mark.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark regains consciousness in pain, sees Mars' horizon stretching below him, and gives the planet his middle finger before contacting Hermes to report broken ribs and that the canvas ripped during ascent.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis confirms the canvas failure and tells Mark they've corrected the intercept range but have a 42 meters per second velocity problem.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark reacts to the bad news about the intercept velocity problem.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Mark interrupts the crew's struggle to find a solution by calling to Lewis.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark proposes puncturing his EVA suit glove to use escaping air as a thruster, directing it with his arm to fly toward Hermes.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis points out the control problems with Mark's plan, noting he'd be eyeballing the intercept with barely controllable thrust, but Mark responds that there are good points to consider.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark comments that he would get to fly around like Iron Man.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis initially dismisses Mark's Iron Man idea but then considers using atmosphere as thrust, ordering Martinez to get Vogel's station running to calculate blowing the VAL.
scenes.turningPointNote: Lewis shifts from rejecting Mark's plan to developing her own dangerous alternative using the VAL explosion

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis realizes they need someone to manually detonate the airlock and orders Vogel to come inside and make a bomb.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark expresses excitement about the crew making a bomb without him.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Vogel confirms he can make a bomb but warns Lewis that setting off explosives on a spacecraft is a terrible idea, then agrees to do it anyway.

scenes.heading: INT. MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lewis announces to Mission Control their plan to deliberately breach the VAL to produce thrust, causing chaos to erupt in the control room.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis orders Beck to place the explosive charge on the inner airlock door while suited up.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck acknowledges the order and heads to his position.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES/MAV - SPACE - INTERCUT
scenes.summary: Mark rips metal scrap from the console and insists on puncturing his suit instead, but Lewis asserts her command authority and refuses to call off the VAL plan.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES REC ROOM - SPACE
scenes.summary: Vogel rapidly constructs a bomb using sugar and liquid oxygen while explaining to Johanssen that it will be eight times more powerful than dynamite.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - VAL (AIRLOCK 1) - SPACE
scenes.summary: Johanssen kisses Beck's face plate before he takes the bomb into the airlock, telling him to be careful and not tell anyone about the kiss.

scenes.heading: INT/EXT. HERMES - VAL (AIRLOCK 1) - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck secures the improvised bomb to the inner airlock door as the outer door opens to space.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Martinez calculates that even with optimal VAL explosion they'll miss the intercept, with Johanssen determining the distance will be 260 meters, prompting Lewis to leave the flight deck.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck climbs along Hermes' hull from Airlock 1 to Airlock 2 using handholds.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis, fully suited, tells Beck she's going untethered because the intercept distance is too far, refusing his offer to do it instead.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: The crew straps in and Johanssen counts down to detonation while Lewis orders them to brace for deceleration.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - VAL (AIRLOCK 1) - SPACE
scenes.summary: The bomb explodes, ripping the airlock door to shreds and slamming Lewis and Beck against the wall as Hermes decelerates.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: The crew endures the deceleration in their chairs until the ship stabilizes, with Johanssen reporting the bridge seal is holding.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis positions herself against the airlock wall and jumps out into space toward Mark.
scenes.turningPointNote: Lewis launches herself into space, beginning the final rescue attempt

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Johanssen reports the intercept range has increased to 312 meters.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark reacts with dark humor to the 312-meter distance, saying he'll wave as he goes by.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis flies through space using her MMU and spots the distant MAV, telling Mark he's still too far and she won't make it.

scenes.heading: INT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark steels himself, unclips his harness, and punctures his suit with the makeshift knife, causing air to shoot out.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark executes his Iron Man plan, puncturing his suit to propel himself toward Lewis

scenes.heading: EXT. MAV - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark launches out of the tumbling MAV and reports visual contact with Lewis as they rocket toward each other through space.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - FLIGHT DECK - SPACE
scenes.summary: Johanssen reports decreasing relative velocity between Mark and Lewis as the crew watches tensely from their stations.

scenes.heading: INT. HERMES - AIRLOCK 2 - SPACE
scenes.summary: Beck watches from Airlock 2 as Johanssen reports the distance to target at 24 meters.

scenes.heading: EXT. HERMES - SPACE
scenes.summary: Lewis fires her MMU thrusters to slow down as Mark approaches, with Johanssen reporting 11 meters to target.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE
scenes.summary: Mark cuts through space with Mars silhouetted behind him as he and Lewis reach toward each other with Johanssen counting down to 6 meters, culminating in Mark announcing contact.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark and Lewis successfully make contact in space, completing the rescue

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark and Lewis float together holding each other, with Mark telling Lewis she has terrible taste in music as he sees the first human in ages.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lewis announces over comms that she got Mark, causing Mission Control to erupt in cheers.

scenes.heading: EXT. ST. PETERSBERG - DAY
scenes.summary: The cheers spread through Russia as the news breaks.

scenes.heading: EXT. TIMES SQUARE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: News reports of Mark's rescue appear on big screens in Times Square.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEIJING - DAY
scenes.summary: The celebration continues in China.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. AIRLOCK - DAY
scenes.summary: Lewis guides Mark to the airlock where the crew waits, and after the airlock cycles, Martinez, Johanssen, and Vogel rush to support the exhausted Mark.

scenes.heading: EXT. EARTH - TO ESTABLISH
scenes.summary: Earth appears warm and welcoming from space.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - JOHNSON SPACE CENTER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark sits alone on a bench at Johnson Space Center, basking in the warmth of a beautiful day, with a title indicating Day 1.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark has returned to Earth, beginning his new life after Mars

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - TEACHING THEATER - DAY
scenes.summary: Mark addresses NASA's Astronaut Candidate Program recruits, joking about surviving on Mars by farming in his own waste.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUNCHPAD - DAY
scenes.summary: A spacecraft is prepared for launch as Annie begins discussing launch procedures.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - PRESS ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Annie briefs the press on the Ares 5 mission while Vincent Kapoor, now Director of Mars Missions, prepares to speak about mission particulars.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL
scenes.summary: Mission Control prepares for launch with Mindy Park as CAPCOM and Brendan Hatch as Flight Director, while Teddy Sanders watches from the viewing room.

scenes.heading: EXT. JPL - MORNING
scenes.summary: Bruce Ng eats breakfast with his team at JPL while watching news reports about the Ares 5 launch, appearing in good spirits.

scenes.heading: INT. SUBURBAN HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Alex Vogel plays with his five blonde children wearing NASA gear in their suburban home during what appears to be a major celebration for the family.

scenes.heading: INT. SPACECRAFT - DAY
scenes.summary: The Ares 5 astronauts secure themselves in their acceleration chairs, with Wen Jiang, the first Chinese national to go to Mars, responding to guidance check.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL
scenes.summary: Chinese officials Zhu Tao and Guo Ming stand proudly in Mission Control listening to Wen run through his checks.

scenes.heading: EXT. PARK - MORNING
scenes.summary: Mitch Henderson watches his grandson play in a park, appearing to be enjoying his forced retirement.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Chris Beck visits his wife Beth Johanssen and their newborn baby girl in the hospital, climbing into bed with them.

scenes.heading: INT. SPACECRAFT - DAY
scenes.summary: Rick Martinez grins with familiar adrenaline as the countdown begins for the Ares 5 launch.

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Melissa Lewis watches the launch coverage on television in her home filled with disco memorabilia and NASA medals while her husband brings her tea.

scenes.heading: INT. NASA - MISSION CONTROL
scenes.summary: Mission Control counts down to launch and erupts in cheers when the spacecraft launches successfully.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE
scenes.summary: The film ends with a view of Earth from space, followed by credits listing the cast and crew.
scenes.turningPointNote: The story concludes with the successful launch of Ares 5, showing humanity's continued exploration of Mars
